body: This product is so weak that one zap only maimed the fly, a small fruit fly not anything huge. After one zap it was flopping around on the floor half dead. I ended up having to get some kitchen roll out to do the job myself 🤢 Also the light function is pointless, it attracts nothing. title: Doesn’t work very well
body: Disappointed. It looks well made. But it doesn’t work well enough. It’s not as good as The executioner. When you swat a fly you need to really get some energy behind the swipe, otherwise the fly just walks on the outside of the metalwork where the electric current isn’t there. You need to have enough pressure in your swing to make the fly go inside the metalwork.The automatic mode doesn’t do anything. The power is on. The light is on. But it doesn’t attract flies even in a dark room. Presumably because the light is so dim.I’d like to return but don’t have original packaging.
date: Reviewed in the United Kingdom on 20 August 2025

author: Stephanie Nelson

title: “Flimsy, Ineffective, and Falls Apart — Don’t Waste Your Money”
body: ⭐️ 1 out of 5 stars — Disappointing and Poorly MadeI really wanted this product to work, but unfortunately it’s a complete letdown. From the moment I started using it, problems began piling up:• The parts literally started falling apart after minimal use. It feels cheaply made and poorly assembled.• Despite the claims, it doesn’t kill flies—even after repeated swings. It just lightly zaps or stuns them (if anything), and they fly away seconds later.• The unit is extremely flimsy and falls over with the slightest bump. It’s not stable at all as a standing bug zapper.• The battery barely holds a charge, making the “rechargeable convenience” completely useless. I’ve had to plug it in constantly, and even then it doesn’t seem to function at full power.Their advertised features like the rotating head, extendable handle, and powerful voltage sound great on paper, but the actual performance doesn’t match the hype. Overall, this was a waste of money. I do not recommend this product.
date: Reviewed in the United States on 2 August 2025

title: Pas facile d'attrapper les mouches !
body: La belle raquette que voilà !On lui demande surtout d'être un outil efficace contre mouches et moustiques et je dirais que si  vous arrivez à les atteindre avant qu'ils s'échappent, alors oui cette raquette est une tueuse.Son seul défaut  en fait c'est son utilisateur car il est très difficile de chopper au vol la mouche qui sera toujours plus rapide que vous.J'ai essayé la méthode statique en fixant la raquette sur son support, batterie chargée à fond . Quelques temps plus-tard  une imprudente vient se coller au grillage de cet engin de mort et la, en deux secondes la malheureuse grésille et flambe en même temps, passant de vie à trépas.Donc vous l'aurez compris ce piège est assez difficile à manipuler  mais d'une façon autonome devient plus efficace, surtout la nuit grâce à la lumière violette de la raquette qui attire les insectes en vol et donc qui viennent se brûler les ailes dessus.D'une manière générale l'outil semble de qualité correcte plutôt solide et facile à recharger avec n'importe quel chargeur que vous aurez sous la main.Précision de dernière minute, le lendemain de cet essai la machine ne répond plus . Plus de contact alors que la charge était complète.
date: Reviewed in France on 14 April 2025

title: Zaps, but doesn't kill fruit flies...?
body: We ordered this when we were overwhelmed with fruit flies we accidentally brought home in a farmer's market haul. The zapper took a while to arrive, but when it did, it always well-packaged and in good working order.The trouble is that it doesn't actually kill fruit flies! We'd hear the zap and see the flies fall from the air, but all of them would continue limping around, stunned, on our counter.They were much easier to kill at that point, but it did feel like a slower and more traumatic death. We can't use it- it feels too cruel.If you're looking to knock bugs out of the air, this is your tool. However, if you're looking for the least inhumane way to eradicate an infestation, this is sadly not it.
date: Reviewed in Canada on 17 November 2025

title: Different version is far, FAR superior
body: Wish there was an option to give this 2.5 stars.So this item is very good for almost every reason.  But it works poorly in one very important way.Before owning this, I owned a somewhat similar, early version.  This new version has a safety feature…the electrified portion of the racket is sandwiched between portions that don’t conduct electricity.  This safety feature acts like a fence and prevents insects from quickly and easily touching the electrified portion!I’m not joking when I say that my previous racket without the safety feature was 100 times more effective at killing insects!!!The safety feature is in the way the vast majority of the time.  It makes the product far less effective!!!Anyone who owns this new version with this “so called safety feature” on it and never used a model without the “safety feature” has no idea how incredibly inefficient it is compared to the older versions!As far as how many times we had problems with people getting electrocuted by our old racket…that never happened.
date: Reviewed in the United States on 24 August 2025

author: Jiaqi Liu

title: Attracts and kills flying insects
body: Updated review: This item was taken out of the sunroom after my review in April, recharged, and put in the kitchen in case of flies. This week was the first time it was used again; when company was in the kitchen and a single fly was being bothersome. I tried unsuccessfully to use the swatter-zapper, but never managed to touch the fly; which strategically stayed very close to the guests. When company was gone  I plugged the zapper in and left it on its stand in the kitchen, hoping the fly would run into it.  Upon returning two hours later, it was still plugged in, but was no longer on.  I've unplugged it, pressed all the buttons many times, plugged it in again, all to no avail.  This unit is dead after very little time or use.Original 4-star 4/11/25 Review: This is doing one heck of a good job at killing moths.  It's shown here in a sunroom, which houses several large potted plants as well as a large bird in a very large cage.  Spring always sees a huge influx of small moths in this room, some of which manage to migrate to the rest of the house.  After fully charging the zapper, I set it upright on an end table in the middle of the room in the evening and turned it on with the light.  By 7:00 a.m. the next day it was out of power, but covered with dead moths as shown.It worked well swatting moths in flight, as long as contact can be kept for a bit.  It did not work to kill moths that were immobile on walls, the ceiling or in corners as the edges of the racquet prevented the wire grid from touching the small moths.Catching the flying insects mid-air is much less messy than swatting them against a wall and the moths shake out easily over a trash can.There's plenty of stored power for hand swatting, where you turn it on and off as you go.  It's best plugged in if keeping it on for hours in its stand.Do be aware that this causes sparks so the instructions aptly warn to keep it away from anything flammable.It's a good product and I look forward to using it on the first fly that gets into the house this season.  It should work even better on flies than on the small, flat moths.
date: Reviewed in the United States on 29 May 2025

author: Donna Adkins

title: Funktioniert nicht immer
body: Im Lieferumfang enthalten sind 2 Fliegenklatschen, 2 Wandhalterungen, 2 Ständer, 2 Streifen Klebeband und ein Ladekabel.Der Sinn der Ständer erschließt sich mir bisher nicht wirklich und die Anleitung ist sehr kurz und erwähnt das Zubehör nicht. Wahrscheinlich soll man das Gerät im Dauerbetrieb aufstellen, um Insekten anzulocken. Das funktioniert aber nicht, das das kleine Licht viel zu schwach ist.Schon das Laden der Geräte stellte mich vor eine Herausforderung. Ich habe sie an ein Standard USB-C Ladekabel angeschlossen und es passierte genau garnichts, obwohl ich mit diesem Kabel normalerweise alle meine kompatiblen Geräte lade. Es tat sich erst etwas, als ich das beiliegende Kabel verwendete (USB-C). Nach ca. einer Stunde leuchtete das Lämpchen grün und es konnte losgehen. Praktisch fand ich, dass sich der Kopf der Klatsche drehen lässt, um es gegen die Wand drücken zu können. Allerdings muss man viel Kraft aufwenden, um den Kopf aus der Verankerung zu lösen.Die Wirkung der Geräte ist eher mäßig, da es nicht immer funktioniert. Mal wird die Mücke gegrillt, mal nicht.Von mir leider keine Kaufempfehlung.
date: Reviewed in Germany on 30 April 2025
author: Nijobe
